signals.title: Fewer storms, not less risk: El Niño will bring mixed results across US power systems
signals.summary: The climate pattern should reduce Atlantic storm activity this year, but utilities still face localized power outage concerns as flooding and wildfires shift to other parts of the country, experts say.

signals.title: Maryland lawmakers back data center transmission cost complaint at FERC
signals.summary: The PJM Interconnection improperly makes ratepayers pay for data center-driven transmission projects that don’t benefit them, according to Maryland's ratepayer advocate.

signals.title: In photos: Star-studded crowd gathers to celebrate the Obama Presidential Center dedication
signals.summary: Barack Obama opened his presidential center in Chicago with former presidents, celebrities and dignitaries in attendance. President Donald Trump was absent.

signals.title: Vance says U.S. isn’t giving Iran ‘a cent’ as he defends Trump peace deal

signals.title: Trump administration temporarily cuts student loan interest rates for borrowers on autopay
signals.summary: The Trump administration announced it will offer federal student loan borrowers who sign up for autopay a larger interest rate discount.

signals.title: U.S. Navy ends blockade of Iran's ports and coastal areas
signals.summary: The Navy on Thursday lifted its blockade of Iran's ports and coastal areas at the direction of President Donald Trump.

signals.title: Hormuz relief may not ease the economic toll that's already 'baked in,' analysts warn

signals.summary: U.S. Pacific Command was the formation's official name until 2018, when it was changed to U.S. Indo-Pacific Command.

signals.summary: General Motors and Rivian announcements this month underscore the growing U.S. electric vehicle fleet’s load management potential.

signals.summary: Ongoing legal battles regarding non-condensing commercial gas water heaters and residential gas furnaces have not yet slowed U.S. gas storage water-heater shipments, AHRI data shows.

signals.title: California gas generation down 60% from 2024 as solar, imports surge

signals.title: Why Japan's $70 billion-plus intervention and a rate hike didn't prop up the yen more
signals.summary: Japan was willing to step in to defend the yen around the 160 level before, and it's at that point again.

signals.title: Yen slides past 161 against the dollar, nearing 40-year low and reviving intervention bets
signals.summary: On Thursday the yen saw a sharp depreciation a high of 161.80, its weakest since July 2024.

signals.title: Indian IT stocks slump up to 7% as Accenture cuts revenue outlook, fueling fresh concerns over sector growth
signals.summary: Shares of major Indian IT companies fall up to 7% after global sector leader Accenture cuts its revenue guidance.

signals.title: MSCI isn't done with Indonesia yet: new report signals continued concerns over market transparency
signals.summary: MSCI flagged growing concerns about transparency in Indonesia's stock market.

signals.title: The average SpaceX buyer post-IPO is almost under water after two-day slide
signals.summary: Shares of SpaceX fell as much as 7% Thursday to $178, leaving the stock trading roughly in line with its volume-weighted average price of just under $180.

signals.title: OPEC chief dismisses IEA supply glut forecast as 'critical' Strait of Hormuz reopens
signals.summary: The IEA said on Wednesday that a lasting resolution to the conflict could drive a surge in supply volumes and trigger a major oil overhang next year.

signals.title: SpaceX stock sinks for a second day as company slips below Amazon in market cap
signals.summary: Investor bullishness showed signs of waning on Wednesday, as shares sank 5%.

signals.title: Japan core inflation holds steady in May, matching expectations despite energy price concerns
signals.summary: Headline inflation edged up to 1.5% from 1.4%, while the so called "core-core" inflation rate, eased to 1.8% from 1.9%.

signals.title: Markets are set for a much more hawkish Warsh Fed than expected
signals.summary: Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh's tough talk on inflation Wednesday reverberated through financial markets.

signals.title: Waymo recalls about 3,900 robotaxis after some drove into 'freeway construction zones'
signals.summary: After some Waymo robotaxis drove into closed construction zones, the company implemented a recall to fix underlying software issues.

signals.title: Bears step up bets against high-yield sector, shaking up bond traders
signals.summary: The iShares iBoxx High Yield Corporate Bond ETF (HYG) saw elevated put volume on Thursday.

signals.title: Boeing demonstrates quantum protocol in payload set for 2027 launch
signals.summary:
PORTLAND, Ore. – Boeing demonstrated a key quantum networking protocol in ground testing of a compact payload ahead of on-orbit experiment in 2027. “High-fidelity entanglement swapping” was demonstrated earlier this […] 
 The post  Boeing demonstrates quantum protocol in payload set for 2027 launch  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Blue Origin begins rebuilding New Glenn pad
signals.summary:
Blue Origin has started rebuilding a launch pad severely damaged in a New Glenn explosion less than three weeks ago as it works to resume launches by year’s end 
 The post  Blue Origin begins rebuilding New Glenn pad  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Relativity Space to privately develop Mars orbiter mission
signals.summary:
Relativity Space plans to launch a Mars orbiter in 2028 as part of a new initiative to privately develop planetary missions. 
 The post  Relativity Space to privately develop Mars orbiter mission  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Upgraded Ariane 6 launches Amazon Leo satellites
signals.summary:
An Ariane 6 with upgraded solid rocket boosters successfully launched three dozen Amazon Leo satellites June 17 as ESA weighs options for increasing the vehicle’s launch rate. 
 The post  Upgraded Ariane 6 launches Amazon Leo satellites  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Germany moves two ships to Djibouti, eyeing multinational Hormuz mission this summer
signals.summary: Germany is among a number of countries that have offered help in securing the shipping lane, but only if an Iran ceasefire deal turns out to be lasting.

signals.title: Read the 14-point memorandum of understanding between the United States and Iran
signals.summary: The 14-point memorandum of understanding between the United States and Iran leaves contentious issues between the countries unresolved.

signals.title: Poland weighs joining X-BAT autonomous vertical-takeoff fighter program
signals.summary: The announcement comes as the Polish government is mulling plans to acquire up to 32 new fighter jets.

signals.title: New 339-mile transmission line brings Canadian hydropower to NYC
signals.summary: The Champlain-Hudson line is expected to meet up to 20% of New York City’s electricity needs, bringing clean energy from Montreal to the Astoria Energy Complex in Queens.

signals.title: China conducts 4 launches in 3 days, but silence follows Kuaizhou–11 launch
signals.summary:
China continued its accelerated launch pace with a series of missions, but long silence followed liftoff of a Kuaizhou-11 solid rocket Wednesday, suggesting potential issues. 
 The post  China conducts 4 launches in 3 days, but silence follows Kuaizhou–11 launch  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Dawn Aerospace raises $25 million
signals.summary:
Dawn Aerospace has raised $25 million to scale up its work in both in-space transportation and suborbital spaceplanes. 
 The post  Dawn Aerospace raises $25 million  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Look Up and Skynopy partner on automated satellite collision avoidance service
signals.summary:
Space surveillance venture Look Up plans to use Skynopy’s ground station network to help automate its proposed low Earth orbit collision avoidance service, the French startups announced June 17. 
 The post  Look Up and Skynopy partner on automated satellite collision avoidance service  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: June 25:  Golden Dome: How Could Sensors Protect the United States?
signals.summary:
Join us as we explore the technologies behind Golden Dome, what’s necessary to make them operate at a high level and what possibilities could be in the works for the satellites involved. 
 The post  June 25:  Golden Dome: How Could Sensors Protect the United States?  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Swiss decision to not contribute to Copernicus tests program’s value model
signals.summary:
MILAN — Switzerland’s decision not to participate in the European Union’s Copernicus Earth observation program during the 2028–2034 funding cycle has revived a broader debate over the value of contributing […] 
 The post  Swiss decision to not contribute to Copernicus tests program’s value model  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: The SpaceNews space unicorn tracker
signals.summary:
Our ongoing list of current private space companies with a valuation of $1 billion or more. 
 The post  The SpaceNews space unicorn tracker  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: US Army’s ‘Tropic Lightning’ Division ponders transformation lessons from Philippine war games
signals.summary: Because of HIMARS, Maj. Gen. Bartholomees pointed out his “division artillery is really what transformed the most.”

signals.title: Size matters: KNDS pitches long-barreled artillery for 60-kilometer base range
signals.summary: The 155mm howitzer is equipped with a girthy 58 caliber gun tube, about 12% longer than the 52 caliber barrels common in Europe.

signals.title: Renault teams up with Thales to boost France’s drone production
signals.summary: The carmaker will build Thales’ Toutatis remotely operated loitering munitions at one of its factories, with estimated production of 1,000 units a month.

signals.title: As Europe rearms, ‘wingman’ aircraft take center stage
signals.summary: European and U.S. defense forces are rapidly developing their own AI-powered drones to complement their fighter jets and carry extra battlefield tech.

signals.title: MBDA advances land version of its naval missile as European strike weapon
signals.summary: Europe lacks long-range strike options that provide deterrence by holding Russian assets at risk, a gap that countries are trying to patch.

signals.title: Rheinmetall pitches shipping container that can spit out swarms of attack drones
signals.summary: One container can hold 18 of the company’s FV-014 munitions, meant to plop out of the container by way of rocket-assisted start.
